explanation: ecrecover returns address(0) instead of reverting when it is given an invalid signature, and this contract uses the recovered address without checking for that sentinel. Raw ecrecover also accepts malleable (high s) signatures, so the same message can have two valid encodings.
impact: If any authorization mapping treats address(0) as a valid or default entry, an attacker can pass a garbage signature, have it recover to address(0), and pass the check, forging approvals, permits, or withdrawals without a real signature.
